name: Good Loans LLC
description_short: Good Loans LLC provides merchant cash advances and working capital loans to small businesses nationwide, approving applicants quickly with flexible repayment terms.
description_long: Good Loans LLC has operated since 2014, positioning itself as an alternative small business lender for entrepreneurs and business owners who may not qualify for traditional bank financing. The company claims to have delivered over $1 billion in funding across thousands of industries and maintains a 95% customer satisfaction rating. They serve a wide range of industries including healthcare, e-commerce, retail, consulting, solar, and agriculture. The company's primary offerings are merchant cash advances and working capital loans, supplemented by credit card processing services and a small business resource center. What distinguishes Good Loans LLC is their stated willingness to approve applicants with less-than-perfect credit when banks decline them, their rapid decision turnaround (decisions in minutes, funding in 24 hours), and their assignment of dedicated account managers to each client. The company emphasizes customized financing solutions and flexible repayment options designed to match business cash flow patterns rather than rigid schedules. However, the website provides minimal transparency regarding actual interest rates, APR ranges, loan terms, or fee structures—information critical for comparing this type of financing. The testimonials, while positive, reference merchant cash advances and do not disclose the true cost of capital or factor rates. As an alternative lender focusing on merchant cash advances (which typically carry factor rates of 1.2 to 1.5x), borrowers should understand that this financing is more expensive than traditional bank loans and SBA programs.
pros: Fast approval process: decisions rendered in minutes with funding in as few as 24 hours
Serves businesses traditional banks reject: explicitly targets applicants with poor credit or limited track records
Dedicated account manager assigned to each client with industry-specific expertise
Flexible repayment options customized to business cash flow and budget
Multiple product offerings: merchant cash advances, working capital loans, and credit card processing
No minimum funding requirement apparent; recent examples show loans from $36,000 to $175,000
Repeat lending capability: testimonials indicate willingness to fund existing clients again during growth phases
cons: No APR, interest rate, or factor rate transparency on website—critical pricing information is withheld until application
Merchant cash advances are inherently expensive financing products (typical factor rates 1.2-1.5x the advance) with daily or weekly repayment structures
Customer testimonials do not disclose true cost of capital or total repayment amounts, making ROI assessment impossible from website alone
Limited regulatory information: no mention of licensing, compliance certifications, or regulatory oversight details
Customer service hours are limited to Pacific Time 7:30am-4:30pm weekdays only, despite serving nationwide businesses
best_for: Business owners with damaged credit who cannot access bank financing but have consistent revenue streams
E-commerce and retail businesses with daily/weekly credit card sales suitable for merchant cash advance repayment
Established small businesses needing fast working capital without lengthy underwriting processes
Industry-specific borrowers (healthcare, trucking, agriculture, food service) seeking lenders experienced in their sector
diagnosis: Good Loans LLC is best for established small business owners with revenue-based sales (retail, food service, e-commerce) who have been declined by banks and need capital quickly, even at higher cost. The critical caveat is that merchant cash advances are significantly more expensive than bank loans or SBA programs; borrowers must request full pricing before committing and understand they are paying a premium for speed and lenient credit requirements.
services: Merchant cash advances with rapid underwriting
Working capital loans for operational expenses
Dedicated account manager support and ongoing client relationship management
Small Business Resource Center (educational content and industry news)
